name: Roast Chicken with Spiced Mushroom and Winter
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Rinse the chicken under cold running water; drain.
3. Season inside of chicken cavity with salt and pepper.
4. Melt 1 tablespoon butter and add 1/4 teaspoon each of cinnamon, cumin, and coriander.
5. Brush mixture over chicken.
6. Tie up legs securely with butcher's string.
7. Place chicken on a rack set into a roasting pan and add 1/2 cup chicken broth.
8. Roast for 30 minutes.
9. While the chicken is roasting, melt 1 tablespoon butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
10. Stir in onion and sauté for 5 minutes, until translucent.
11. Add remaining spices; cook for about 1 minute, until aroma is released.
12. Add mushrooms, parsnips, and carrots; cook until all vegetables are tender and any excess liquid has evaporated, about 6 minutes.
13. Remove from heat and stir in hazelnuts, bread crumbs, and remaining chicken broth; add salt and pepper.
14. Place the mixture in a ceramic or glass casserole dish.
15. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F.
16. Continue to roast chicken for another hour and 15 minutes, until meat thermometer registers 180°F when inserted between thigh and breast.
17. Cook stuffing during last half of roasting time.
18. Check the roasting pan periodically as the chicken cooks; if it becomes dry, add water, several tablespoons at a time.
19. Remove chicken to a serving platter and cover with foil.
20. Skim fat off juices left in roasting pan.
21. Set pan over low heat on hot plate; add sherry, stirring to scrape off any bits of chicken stuck to the pan.
22. Stir in flour to slightly thicken; simmer for about 1 minute.
23. Pour pan juices into a gravy boat or small pitcher.
24. Uncover chicken; remove string from legs; carve and serve with gravy and stuffing.
Makes 6 servings.
